reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome BEFORE building — the real ONNX graph flattened a synthetically-warped page back to a clean rectangle, visually screenshot-confirmed; then the full demo was driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver across all 5 pages; desktop+mobile 0px overflow measured), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21, WASM, fresh profile) BEFORE building. The real UVDoc ONNX graph (PaddlePaddle/UVDoc_onnx, single-file inference.onnx ~30 MB) was loaded via ort.InferenceSession.create. inputNames=['image'], outputNames=['fetch_name_0']; output dims [1,3,H,W]. On a synthetically curled+perspective-warped Wikipedia page the output FLATTENED the page to a clean rectangle (a side-by-side screenshot showed straightened text lines and squared edges), RMSE(out, warped-input) 0.19 — a real geometric correction, NOT identity, NOT noise. Then the deployed demo ran end-to-end: Flatten produced a rectified page at inference 833 ms + composite 3 ms.
rubric.notes: Inference AND the dense RGBA composite run off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; the flattened ImageBitmap is transferred (not cloned) back.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = the geometrically rectified page [1,3,H,W] in [0,1], the unwarped image directly (UVDoc outputs the flattened page, not a deformation grid, so no grid_sample post-step is needed). Same spatial size as the (multiple-of-32-rounded) input. Semantic sanity: on an already-flat page correction ≈ small; on a warped page the correction RMS rises (0.11-0.19 measured) and the visible geometry straightens — it is a real deformation inversion, not a crop or a rotate.
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT capability vs every built vision demo: document geometric dewarping/rectification. Not built before; distinct from the built restoration (deblur/dehaze/low-light/denoise/super-res/inpaint), depth, segmentation and detection demos.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (PaddlePaddle/UVDoc_onnx, linked), the task (image-to-image / document dewarping), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21 — noting transformers.js has no UVDoc class/task), the real I/O tensor names + shapes (image [1,3,H,W] / fetch_name_0 [1,3,H,W]), quantisation (fp32 single file), backend (WASM), download (~30 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). No invented API surface.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only (the isolated per-worker ORT-web escape hatch, like nafnet/iat/lama/microdehaze) — shared lib/webai.js is untouched.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'PaddlePaddle/UVDoc_onnx', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:30}). The model is fetched THROUGH Cache Storage (cache name uvdoc-onnx-cache) so lib/model-cache.js scans '/PaddlePaddle/UVDoc_onnx/' and a returning visit auto-initialises with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~30 MB). Verified in headless Chrome: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it downloaded and flipped the loader to ready. A per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.
rubric.notes: Never silently re-downloads; the ~30 MB blob is streamed with progress.

rubric.evidence: Loading/progress/ready/error states are announced in role=status aria-live regions (shared loader + page #status). Download progress streams a percent. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Flattening failed: …'); the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path. No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere (conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds).
rubric.notes: Measured 0 console errors and 0 network failures on load AND run across all 5 pages.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the curl/skew-amount range slider (live-updating the preview), the accessible before/after reveal range (aria-valuetext '% flattened'), the sample gallery (rights-safe document thumbnails as labelled buttons), the upload/drop dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space, drag+drop), the Flatten button, and a Download-flattened anchor. Ladder pages add: wild's fold-severity push with a verdict; multi-model's DBNet text-detection chain.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built nafnet/iat/microdehaze image-to-image demos; the reveal wipe uses a native range input for full keyboard support.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real UVDoc ONNX graph and the displayed flattened page + correction number are all derived from the real fetch_name_0 tensor. The synthetic curl/skew is applied transparently on the client (the user chooses the warp and watches it undone, exactly like nafnet's synthetic blur / dehaze's synthetic haze); uploaded document photos are treated as already-warped and passed straight through.
rubric.notes: Honest by construction: 'Add curl/skew' is disclosed in every label; a real warped upload skips it entirely.

rubric.evidence: All canvases carry role=img + descriptive aria-labels (warped input / flattened output / see-inside pair); every button has an accessible name (conformance buttons-have-names pass); the loader status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext. Light+dark inherit the WCAG-AA design system. Gallery thumbnails are real buttons with aria-pressed + license attribution on selection.
rubric.notes: Consulted modern-web-guidance for accessible live-region status + exposing canvas content; applied.

rubric.evidence: Mobile+desktop MATRIX measured 0 px horizontal overflow on ALL 5 pages at mobile (360×740, DPR3) AND desktop (1280×800). The two-col layout is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; the param-grid is auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); reveal/canvases are inline-size:100%; readout wraps. scripts/responsive-check on the index reported desktop clean · mobile clean.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok. Mobile download cost (~30 MB fp32) surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the dense-output canvas composite run in the Web Worker; the flattened ImageBitmap is transferred, not cloned (invariant 15's dense-output-off-main-thread rule). The main thread only draws bounded fit-canvases + the reveal composite. A pass is ~0.83 s on single-thread WASM; the UI never blocks — INP stays low.
rubric.notes: The synthetic warp is a bounded strip-draw on a ≤512-px working copy, redrawn only on slider input.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy image work off main thread web worker; dense output composite without blocking
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks; break up / offload heavy synchronous processing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference AND the dense composite run in a dedicated module Web Worker; the flattened ImageBitmap is transferred back (zero-copy). The main thread does only bounded canvas draws.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([flatBmp]); UI responsive during flattening; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive two column control panel with canvas image output and before/after reveal without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing, logical properties and flexible tracks (fr/minmax) before fixed widths; content-first flexbox with flex-wrap; use min-inline-size:0 so flex children can shrink — fewer media queries, no overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — two-col is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; param-grid is grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); reveal/canvases inline-size:100% block-size:auto; logical properties throughout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px horizontal overflow measured on all 5 pages at mobile 360×740 DPR3 AND desktop 1280×800

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for model loading progress; expose canvas-rendered content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; canvases carry role=img + aria-label; the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext; every button has a name.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; canvas aria-labels present

openQuestions: The bundled samples are flat screenshots + a synthetic curl; a genuinely photographed curled document (rights-safe) would show UVDoc at its best (it is trained on real page photos, so a synthetic warp of an already-flat screenshot is a slightly out-of-distribution input).
UVDoc outputs the flattened image directly; a variant that also returns the deformation grid could enable overlaying the correction field in 'see inside'.

followUpGoals.goal: Add one or two rights-safe REAL photographed warped-document samples to media/manifest.json (with full provenance) so the dewarping gallery shows genuine page curl, not only a synthetic one.
Add a dewarp -> OCR (recognition, not just detection) multi-model demo once a browser-runnable OCR recognizer is chained, to show end-to-end 'photo of a page -> searchable text'.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for uvdoc-document-dewarping (PaddlePaddle/UVDoc_onnx, image-to-image document dewarping, fp32 single-file ~30 MB — a DISTINCT vision capability not previously built: geometric rectification of a warped page). Shipped as the sanctioned substitute for the requested shadow-removal target, which was recorded status:blocked (no single-image shadow-removal model ships a browser-runnable ONNX — all are pytorch/.pkl; the only 'shadow' ONNX are a security-backdoor PoC and an anime scorer; the nearest lighting model, PIE-Net delighting, is ~800 MB + license-unclear + intrinsic-decomposition, not honest cast-shadow removal). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED FIRST in headless Chrome: the real ONNX graph flattened a synthetically curled+perspective-warped page to a clean rectangle (visual screenshot confirmed), RMSE(out,warped) 0.19, not identity. transformers.js has no UVDoc class, so it runs the ONNX directly via a per-worker onnxruntime-web pin. Full demo driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver: Download ~30 MB -> auto-init ready -> real output on the index + all 4 ladder pages (basics/practical flatten + PNG download; wild fold-severity breaking-point; multi-model chained DBNet text detection, boxes axis-aligned on the flattened page). responsive-check 0 px overflow on all 5 pages at desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Ladder: basics (curl then flatten), practical (flatten a real page + download the scan), wild (extreme-fold breaking point), multi-model (dewarp -> text detection). License Apache-2.0.
